scope: manual competitive rewrite candidates from angular importance-3 batch 01

reasons: the answer explains the legacy NgModule pattern well but underplays how much less central it is in standalone-first Angular
follow-up coverage on providedIn versus forRoot is weaker than the official singleton-services guidance
the answer needs a sharper modern-context note so it feels current instead of only historically correct
items.recommendedAction: if revisited later, add one early block that frames forRoot and forChild as a mostly legacy NgModule pattern, then contrast router setup with the modern providedIn and provideRouter defaults
